title: remote python client for podman
body:
* Use podman library for access
* Verbose error checking
* Planned windows and macosx ports

Signed-off-by: Jhon Honce <jhonce@redhat.com>

comments.body: how would you feel about adding a README.md to this with a brief how to use it?
would you consider using an entrypoint in setup.py?  something like -> https://github.com/projectatomic/registries/blob/master/setup.py#L12
couple of comments, LGTM, nice work @jwhonce

What is the command used to run this?  Could we get at least a simple man page.

pypodman would be my preferred command name?  But I am willing to look at others.
Also want to have a

make install.python 
Option to install this.
@baude @rhatdan Agree to all.  I wanted to get this in front of people early. I added WIP to mark it as such.
@jwhonce Looks good, and I would like to merge this to allow people to play with it.  But we need an easy way to install and execute the command.
yeah what he said ... id like to test before i hit the road too, maybe in osx for you
@baude last commit has setup.py entry-point configured

@jwhonce Conflicts?
Remove the [WIP] on the PR, and lets get this in.
@jwhonce Seems to be broken in travis?

:pushpin: Commit fc42cdb has been approved by `rhatdan`

<!-- @rh-atomic-bot r=rhatdan fc42cdb3409cdf18ae73d84f24dc10845171c9c5 -->
I'm going to give it 5 minutes and force-merge this and #1089 so I can cut a release
setup.py install --root $BUILDROOT seems to install `/usr/lib/python3.6/site-packages/lib` <- which seems wrong, it should atleast be in /usr/lib/python3.6/site-packages/podman/lib or something

@jwhonce would it make sense to move `contrib/python/pypodman/lib` to say `contrib/python/pypodman/pypodman/libs` ... kinda like what's being done for `contrib/python/podman/podman/libs`. I think that'd make packaging easier.
